name: Seaside Jawari
description: This algorithm is based on the open source Jawari VCV Rack module by Seaside Modular. It is a simulation of the tanpura, the Indian drone instrument. The tanpura is a four-stringed instrument, without frets. The four strings are typically played in sequence, in a continuous loop, and this is what this algorithm does. Each time it gets a strum (from the strum input bus, or via the strum parameter) it advances to and plays the next string. The second, third, and fourth strings of the tanpura are generally tuned in octaves, with the fourth string an octave below the other two. The first string is tuned to another note of the scale, very often the fifth. For a completely authentic sound, you might consider using the microtuning support to tune the strings to just intervals.
gui_description: The display shows the tuning of the four strings, with the last string played highlighted.

input_ports.name: Strum
input_ports.description: The bus to use for a strum trigger.

input_ports.name: Bridge shape
input_ports.description: The bus to use for CV control over the bridge shape.

input_ports.name: Reset
input_ports.description: The bus to use for a reset trigger.

input_ports.name: V/oct main
input_ports.description: The bus to use for pitch control over the instrument as a whole.

input_ports.name: V/oct 1st string
input_ports.description: The bus to use for pitch control over the tuning of the first string.
